incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect tiles on first attempt for row equation

steps.text: Not quite. Count the rows — the horizontal bands.

incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect tiles on second attempt for row equation

params.description: One row pulses to show what counts as a row.

steps.text: Let's think about this together. The rows are highlighted. There are 6 rows. Each row has 7 dots.

incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect tiles three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System places tiles 6, 7, and 42 into slots.

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. I count the rows: 6. I count the dots in one row: 7. So the equation is 6 times 7 equals 42.

incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect tiles on first attempt for column equation

steps.text: Check the columns — the vertical bands.

incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect tiles on second attempt for column equation

params.description: One column pulses to show what counts as a column.

steps.text: Here's a hint: the columns are highlighted. Count them. There are 7 columns. Each column has 6 dots.

incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect tiles three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System places tiles 7, 6, and 42 into slots.

steps.text: Here, let me walk you through this one. I count the columns: 7. I count the dots in one column: 6. So the equation is 7 times 6 equals 42. The product stays the same.
